assertMatrix.comment: The score viewer lazy-loads OpenSheetMusicDisplay, so /play pages ship a much larger script payload than the rest of the app. Budget them apart so the OSMD bulk can't hide an app-code regression on the lighter pages, and the lighter pages' tight cap still trips on a real one. Lighthouse builds a single locale (per-visitor weight); this cap sits just under the former all-locales figure and can tighten further toward the measured per-locale number.

Every indexable page that does not load the OSMD vendor chunk (the personal you/settings pages are matched separately below). The script cap is a tight ratchet on the shared/app code shipped to a normal page. Lighthouse builds a single locale (per-visitor weight); this cap sits just under the former all-locales figure and can tighten further toward the measured per-locale number.

The personal/utility pages (You, Settings) carry a deliberate noindex, so Lighthouse's is-crawlable audit tanks their SEO category by design — turn that assertion off for them while keeping the same performance, layout-shift, best-practices, and script-size gates as the other non-OSMD pages. They stay in the audit list for that coverage.
